topic: Borrowable patterns from Overstory swarm orchestration system

sources.description: SQLite mail system - schema, message types, WAL config
FIFO merge queue - SQLite, status tracking, dequeue atomicity
4-tier conflict resolution - clean, auto, AI, reimagine
Transcript parser - token extraction, model-specific pricing
cnogo memory engine - SQLite schema, CRUD, sync, worktree
cnogo hooks - token telemetry, secret scanning, post-edit format
cnogo skills library - 15 domain and workflow skills
cnogo agent definitions - implementer, debugger, resolver

summary: Overstory's SQLite mail system provides persistent, queryable inter-agent messaging with broadcast groups -- cnogo currently relies on ephemeral Claude Code native messaging
4-tier merge conflict resolution (clean -> auto-resolve -> AI -> reimagine) catches 60-80% of conflicts mechanically before agent escalation -- cnogo has no automatic tiers
Watchdog daemon with progressive escalation (warn -> nudge -> escalate -> terminate) detects stuck agents -- cnogo has staleIndicatorMinutes config but no implementation
Transcript-based cost tracking with model-specific pricing enables per-agent cost attribution -- cnogo only has generic token estimation
Unified doctor command runs all health checks in one pass -- cnogo has validation scattered across multiple scripts
Overstory's own STEELMAN.md warns that swarms rarely outperform single-agent workflows for interconnected work, validating cnogo's conservative small-batch approach
recommendation: Adopt 3 patterns immediately: (1) tiered merge resolution (tiers 1-2 only) in worktree.py, (2) unified /doctor diagnostic command, (3) transcript-based cost tracking. Defer agent health monitoring until hang frequency is quantified. Skip SQLite mail and capability-based access control -- Claude Code native features cover these adequately.
